question: In foggy conditions, which lights should a driver use?

answers.text: Low beam headlights.
answers.explanation: Utilizing low beam headlights in foggy conditions ensures better visibility as high beams can reflect off the fog and impair your vision.

answers.text: High beam headlights.
Parking lights.

explanation: Utilizing low beam headlights in foggy conditions ensures better visibility as high beams can reflect off the fog and impair your vision.

question: On a road with traffic moving in both directions, how should the center lane be utilized?

answers.text: For passing slower vehicles
To prepare for a left-hand turn
answers.explanation: The shared center lane is intended only for vehicles preparing to make a safe left-hand turn. It should not be used for passing or through traffic.

answers.text: For through-travel when lanes are congested
For parking temporarily

explanation: The shared center lane is intended only for vehicles preparing to make a safe left-hand turn. It should not be used for passing or through traffic.

question: What is the correct order of actions as a school bus prepares to pick up or drop off children?

answers.text: Yellow lights on the bus start flashing, then red lights flash, a stop sign extends, and the bus stops with doors open for children.
answers.explanation: When approaching a school bus with flashing yellow lights, reduce your speed to no more than 20 mph and prepare to stop. If the bus has red flashing lights or an extended stop sign, stop at least 15 feet away from the bus and remain stopped as long as the red lights are flashing or the stop sign is out.

answers.text: The bus driver first uses red flashing lights, then yellow flashing lights when children are seated or unloaded.
The bus stops with a stop sign out and yellow lights flashing to either load or unload children.

explanation: When approaching a school bus with flashing yellow lights, reduce your speed to no more than 20 mph and prepare to stop. If the bus has red flashing lights or an extended stop sign, stop at least 15 feet away from the bus and remain stopped as long as the red lights are flashing or the stop sign is out.

question: What is the appropriate response if your car's wheels accidentally drop off the edge of the road?

answers.text: Swiftly turn the steering wheel to re-enter the road.
Increase your speed and merge back onto the road smoothly.
Apply your brakes firmly until you come to a stop.
Reduce your speed gradually by gently braking. Steer back onto the pavement when it is safe to do so.
answers.explanation: If your vehicle goes off the roadway, you should slow down by gently braking and steer back onto the pavement only when safe. A sudden sharp turn of the steering wheel can cause loss of control.

explanation: If your vehicle goes off the roadway, you should slow down by gently braking and steer back onto the pavement only when safe. A sudden sharp turn of the steering wheel can cause loss of control.

question: Safety zones are designated for pedestrian use around buses or trolleys. When is it permissible to drive through a safety zone?

answers.text: Only when a bus or trolley is approaching.
Only when a bus or trolley is stopped.
Under no circumstances.
answers.explanation: Safety zones are designated areas for pedestrian use; driving through them is prohibited at all times.

explanation: Safety zones are designated areas for pedestrian use; driving through them is prohibited at all times.

question: In foggy weather, which lights should you use for better visibility?

answers.text: High beams.
Low beams.
answers.explanation: Low beams create less glare and improve visibility during foggy conditions.

answers.text: Emergency blinkers.

explanation: Low beams create less glare and improve visibility during foggy conditions.

question: When traveling at a speed of 60 mph, how much is a driver's field of vision reduced?

answers.text: Half of what it is when the vehicle is not moving.
One-fourth of what it is when the vehicle is not moving.
answers.explanation: At higher speeds, a driver's field of vision narrows significantly. When traveling at 60 mph, it reduces to one-fourth of the field of vision when stationary.

answers.text: One-third of what it is when the vehicle is not moving.
Two-thirds of what it is when the vehicle is not moving.

explanation: At higher speeds, a driver's field of vision narrows significantly. When traveling at 60 mph, it reduces to one-fourth of the field of vision when stationary.

question: What should you do if pedestrians are close to the roadway?

answers.text: Honk your horn and proceed at the same speed.
Accelerate to move through the area faster.
Reduce speed and proceed carefully.
answers.explanation: When you see pedestrians nearby, you should slow down and carefully pass by. Always be ready to stop if necessary to ensure their safety.

explanation: When you see pedestrians nearby, you should slow down and carefully pass by. Always be ready to stop if necessary to ensure their safety.

question: What should you do if a barrier is lowering at a drawbridge?

answers.text: Try to go around the barrier.
Speed up to pass under the barrier before it closes.
Stop completely and wait until the barrier is lifted.
answers.explanation: It is important to come to a full stop and wait for the barrier to rise, as disobeying the gate can be both illegal and highly dangerous.

answers.text: Stop only if you are unable to pass under the barrier in time.

explanation: It is important to come to a full stop and wait for the barrier to rise, as disobeying the gate can be both illegal and highly dangerous.

question: On average, how many alcoholic beverages can a person's body process in one hour?

answers.text: Half a drink.
One drink.
answers.explanation: On average, it takes the body about an hour to metabolize the alcohol in one drink.

answers.text: Two drinks.
Three drinks.

explanation: On average, it takes the body about an hour to metabolize the alcohol in one drink.

question: For environmental reasons, how long is it recommended that you let your vehicle idle?

answers.text: Three minutes.
answers.explanation: To reduce emissions, vehicles should not idle for more than this time duration.

answers.text: Five minutes.
Ten minutes.
Fifteen minutes.

explanation: To reduce emissions, vehicles should not idle for more than this time duration.

question: If you encounter a flashing red traffic signal, you should:

answers.text: Continue through the intersection if no vehicles are present.
Proceed with caution, giving way to all other traffic.
Stop and only proceed when safe to do so.
Treat it as a stop sign.
answers.explanation: At a flashing red light, drivers must treat it the same as a stop sign.

explanation: At a flashing red light, drivers must treat it the same as a stop sign.

question: Under which condition are you allowed to use the right shoulder to pass another vehicle?

answers.text: It is never permitted.
answers.explanation: Driving on the shoulder to pass another vehicle is not allowed under any circumstances, as it is illegal and unsafe.

answers.text: Only when it's an emergency.
If the car ahead is moving too slowly.
While the car in front is turning left.

explanation: Driving on the shoulder to pass another vehicle is not allowed under any circumstances, as it is illegal and unsafe.

question: Should drivers refrain from using mobile phones and other devices while driving unless reporting an emergency?

answers.text: True
answers.explanation: Operating a vehicle requires your undivided attention, so using phones or other electronic devices while driving is unsafe and should be avoided, except in emergencies.

answers.text: False

explanation: Operating a vehicle requires your undivided attention, so using phones or other electronic devices while driving is unsafe and should be avoided, except in emergencies.

question: To maintain control on wet road surfaces, you should:

answers.text: Turn sharply to avoid water.
Brake hard and continuously.
Reduce your speed.
answers.explanation: Slowing down helps maintain traction on wet roads by reducing the chance of hydroplaning. Steer gently and keep tires properly inflated.

explanation: Slowing down helps maintain traction on wet roads by reducing the chance of hydroplaning. Steer gently and keep tires properly inflated.

question: During freezing wet conditions, which area is most prone to hiding ice?

answers.text: Curves in the road
Bridges and elevated roadways
answers.explanation: Elevated structures freeze more quickly and may have hidden icy patches.

answers.text: City streets during rush hour

explanation: Elevated structures freeze more quickly and may have hidden icy patches.

question: If you are feeling stressed, anxious, or upset, you:

answers.text: Should allow another person to take over driving.
answers.explanation: It's safest for someone else to drive if you're emotional.

answers.text: Will have heightened awareness and drive more carefully.
Will drive as they normally do, unaffected by emotions.

explanation: It's safest for someone else to drive if you're emotional.

question: On a poorly lit road at night, how should you adjust your driving?

answers.text: Ensure you are able to halt within the distance that your headlights illuminate.
answers.explanation: Driving slowly enough to stop within the visible area of your headlights is crucial for safety during night driving.

answers.text: Use your high beams all the time to see further ahead.
Set the dashboard lights to their brightest setting to be seen by others.

explanation: Driving slowly enough to stop within the visible area of your headlights is crucial for safety during night driving.

question: What should you do if a vehicle is tailgating you?

answers.text: Speed up to increase the gap.
Flash your brake lights and continue as usual.
Lightly tap your brakes, reduce speed, and allow them to overtake.
answers.explanation: If another car is tailgating you, lightly tap your brakes to signal them, then slow down to let them pass safely. If this doesn't work, pull over and stop if necessary.

answers.text: Drive in the middle of the lane to block them.

explanation: If another car is tailgating you, lightly tap your brakes to signal them, then slow down to let them pass safely. If this doesn't work, pull over and stop if necessary.

question: How should you adjust your following distance when traveling behind a motorcycle?

answers.text: Remain consistent with the distance kept from other types of vehicles.
Be shorter than for larger vehicles.
Be greater than for other vehicles.
answers.explanation: When trailing a motorcycle, increase your following distance. Motorcycles can stop and maneuver more rapidly than larger vehicles, so extra space is essential to react safely to their quick changes.

explanation: When trailing a motorcycle, increase your following distance. Motorcycles can stop and maneuver more rapidly than larger vehicles, so extra space is essential to react safely to their quick changes.

question: When is it safe to return to the right lane after passing a vehicle?

answers.text: You can see the car you passed in your rearview mirror.
answers.explanation: Before moving back into the right lane, ensure you can see the front of the passed vehicle in your interior rearview mirror. Also, glance over your right shoulder to confirm there is a safe distance between your car and the vehicle you passed.

answers.text: Your turn signal is still blinking.
Other vehicles start to flash their headlights.

explanation: Before moving back into the right lane, ensure you can see the front of the passed vehicle in your interior rearview mirror. Also, glance over your right shoulder to confirm there is a safe distance between your car and the vehicle you passed.

question: What is the legal implication of fleeing an accident scene?

answers.text: Strongly advised against but not illegal.
A necessary action if no one is hurt.
A criminal offense.
answers.explanation: Leaving the scene of an accident is considered a serious crime.

answers.text: Permissible under certain conditions.

explanation: Leaving the scene of an accident is considered a serious crime.

question: When you encounter orange cones and construction signs on a highway, what should you do?

answers.text: Slow down because there is a lane closure ahead.
Watch for workers and machinery on the roadway.
answers.explanation: In a construction area, signs and orange cones alert you to the presence of workers and equipment, requiring reduced speed and increased caution.

answers.text: Continue at the same speed in your current lane.

explanation: In a construction area, signs and orange cones alert you to the presence of workers and equipment, requiring reduced speed and increased caution.

question: What is something you should avoid doing when changing lanes?

answers.text: Fail to signal before making the lane change.
answers.explanation: Ensure to signal your intentions to other drivers before changing lanes.

answers.text: Suddenly swerve into another lane without warning.
answers.explanation: Changing lanes abruptly can cause accidents. Always check mirrors, blind spots, and signal before safely moving lanes.

answers.text: Ensure the new lane is clear of other vehicles before switching.
answers.explanation: Verify that the lane is free of other vehicles and hazards before making a lane change.

explanation: Changing lanes abruptly can cause accidents. Always check mirrors, blind spots, and signal before safely moving lanes.

question: Are vehicles required to have their taillights on during nighttime driving?

answers.text: True
False
answers.explanation: Vehicles must have their headlights turned on from sunset to sunrise and must have two red taillights visible from 500 feet to the rear. Mopeds and motorcycles need only one taillight.

explanation: Vehicles must have their headlights turned on from sunset to sunrise and must have two red taillights visible from 500 feet to the rear. Mopeds and motorcycles need only one taillight.

question: Which substances can impair your driving capabilities?

answers.text: Prescribed medications only
All types of medications
answers.explanation: Any type of medication, whether over-the-counter or prescribed, can impair your ability to drive. Always read the warning labels and know how the medication affects you before deciding to drive.

answers.text: Alcohol and illegal substances

explanation: Any type of medication, whether over-the-counter or prescribed, can impair your ability to drive. Always read the warning labels and know how the medication affects you before deciding to drive.

question: What happens when driving at high speeds on a wet road?

answers.text: Increasing your speed reduces your tires' ability to channel water out of the way.
answers.explanation: Driving faster on wet roads can cause your tires to lose their effectiveness in diverting water, increasing the risk of hydroplaning.

answers.text: High-quality tires are unaffected by wet roadways.
It is less risky to drive through deeper water than shallow water.
Slowing down makes the wet road more dangerous.

explanation: Driving faster on wet roads can cause your tires to lose their effectiveness in diverting water, increasing the risk of hydroplaning.

question: What is the purpose of broken yellow lines on streets and highways?

answers.text: Mark the edge of the roadway.
Indicate where turning is allowed.
Divide lanes of traffic traveling in opposite directions.
answers.explanation: Broken yellow lines separate traffic moving in opposite directions and allow passing when it is safe.

explanation: Broken yellow lines separate traffic moving in opposite directions and allow passing when it is safe.

question: When driving in the rain, when are roads most slippery?

answers.text: When the rain has been continuous for several hours.
A half-hour after the rain ceases.
In the initial 30 minutes of rainfall.
answers.explanation: The roads are most slippery during the first 30 minutes of rain because the water mixes with oil residue, creating a slick surface.

explanation: The roads are most slippery during the first 30 minutes of rain because the water mixes with oil residue, creating a slick surface.

question: Under what circumstances should parking lights be utilized?

answers.text: While driving at night with headlights off.
answers.explanation: Using parking lights while driving without headlights at night is unsafe.

answers.text: When your car is stationary.
answers.explanation: Parking lights are intended for use when your car is stopped and parked.

answers.text: When following other vehicles closely.
answers.explanation: Parking lights are not meant for use when actively driving.

answers.text: When driving during foggy conditions.

explanation: Parking lights are intended for use when your car is stopped and parked.

question: What is the proper procedure for performing a three-point turn?

answers.text: Give a signal, glance over your shoulder, and then proceed with the turnabout.
Signal and complete the turnabout quickly.
Turn on your signal, check your mirrors, look over your shoulder, and only proceed with the turnabout when the road is fully clear.
answers.explanation: To safely perform a three-point turn, ensure the road is clear by signaling, checking mirrors, and looking over your shoulder before the maneuver.

explanation: To safely perform a three-point turn, ensure the road is clear by signaling, checking mirrors, and looking over your shoulder before the maneuver.

question: What does it signify if the center of the road has one solid yellow line and one broken yellow line next to your lane?

answers.text: Passing is prohibited in this zone under any conditions.
You may overtake other vehicles on the left when it is safe, as long as there is no incoming traffic.
answers.explanation: A broken yellow line alongside your lane allows for passing if it is safe and there is no oncoming traffic.

answers.text: The area is designated for right turns only.

explanation: A broken yellow line alongside your lane allows for passing if it is safe and there is no oncoming traffic.

question: When planning to overtake another car, you should:

answers.text: Do not expect the other driver to create a gap for you to reenter your lane.
answers.explanation: You cannot rely on others altering their speed or position safely.

answers.text: Expect the other driver to allow you to pass if you signal.
Assume the other driver will keep the same speed.

explanation: You cannot rely on others altering their speed or position safely.

question: When your vehicle's wheels drift off the road onto the shoulder, you should slow down gradually before steering back onto the road.

answers.text: True
answers.explanation: If your car's wheels veer off the roadway onto the shoulder, hold the steering wheel steadily, ease off the gas, and gently brake. Carefully check traffic before steering back onto the road.

answers.text: False

explanation: If your car's wheels veer off the roadway onto the shoulder, hold the steering wheel steadily, ease off the gas, and gently brake. Carefully check traffic before steering back onto the road.

question: When driving over 30 mph, what is the minimum safe following distance you should maintain?

answers.text: At least 1 second.
At least 2 seconds.
At least 3 seconds.
At least 4 seconds.
answers.explanation: A four-second gap allows for safe stopping distance and reaction time.

explanation: A four-second gap allows for safe stopping distance and reaction time.

question: What action should you take when you see a solid yellow traffic light?

answers.text: Speed up to pass through before it turns red.
Keep driving because you still have the right-of-way.
Prepare to stop unless you're already in the intersection.
answers.explanation: A steady yellow traffic light warns that the light will soon turn red. You should stop if it is safe to do so, unless you are already in the intersection.

answers.text: Observe other cars to see their actions.

explanation: A steady yellow traffic light warns that the light will soon turn red. You should stop if it is safe to do so, unless you are already in the intersection.
